steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Ezeulu 'considered the immensity of his power' and then 'wondered if it was real.' The blank describes deep, sustained reflection that leads to doubt — not expectation, permission, or admiration.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'pondered': means to think carefully and at length. 'Whenever Ezeulu pondered the immensity of his power…he wondered if it was real' — deep reflection producing doubt fits exactly. ✓
  • Choice B — 'anticipated': means looked forward to something future. Ezeulu already holds this power; he is not expecting it. ✗
  • Choice C — 'allowed': means permitted. Ezeulu does not permit his power to exist — he reflects on it. ✗
  • Choice D — 'respected': means held in high regard. If he respected his power, he would not doubt whether it was real. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
